Event: Marries his former step-sister during his presidential campaign
Explanation: Creates serious taboo scandal risk with minimal shown upside beyond a poll bump rooted in public perversity.

Event: Hits on Amy, Sue, and every remotely attractive woman he meets
Explanation: He seeks personal gratification but is always rebuffed and makes women uncomfortable.

Event: Frequently makes unintentionally misogynistic remarks
Explanation: Reinforces his unpopularity and harms women’s comfort with no benefit.

Event: Reluctantly admits Teddy molested him to Kent and Eric
Explanation: His disclosure gains him little and exposes vulnerability while giving others important information they ignore.

Event: Tries to talk to Catherine about Teddy molesting him to avoid looking like a loser
Explanation: Reopens his trauma and confuses Catherine without improving his reputation.

Event: Walks out on class action lawsuit against Teddy because other victims are women
Explanation: Undercuts his own legal/justice interests and weakens the case for other victims.

Event: Makes Islamophobic remarks that gain him popularity with bigots
Explanation: Boosts his political following while fueling prejudice and social harm.

Event: Demands Selina show her birth certificate to prove her age
Explanation: Gains attention and conspiracist support while degrading norms and Selina’s position.

Event: Runs for President on an anti-immigrant platform
Explanation: Improves his political fortunes by stoking hostility toward immigrants.

Event: Recognizes some followers’ “murder illegals” rhetoric is too extreme and admits he went too far
Explanation: Walks back the most violent rhetoric, modestly helping others and slightly hurting his extremist appeal.

Event: Has friendly, positive banter with the woman with Down syndrome at the bowling alley
Explanation: Builds rapport that helps his image and genuinely treats her well.

Event: When video makes it look like he yelled at the woman, worries first about whether she will believe it
Explanation: Prioritizes her feelings over campaign optics, modestly helping both.

Event: Realizes he’s at a neo-Nazi rave and immediately leaves
Explanation: Protects himself from association while reducing his complicity in an extremist event.

Event: Campaigns on an anti-Meyer platform after being sick of Selina bossing him
Explanation: Improves his polling and leverage while undercutting Selina and adding toxicity to politics.

Event: Hires Teddy, who sexually harassed him, to mock Teddy for chemical castration
Explanation: Gains petty emotional satisfaction at Teddy’s expense while reintroducing his abuser into his orbit.

Event: Moves up the political ladder and becomes more unhinged and vile
Explanation: Accrues more power while increasingly harming the public sphere.

Event: Hates Daylight Savings Time and campaigns against it
Explanation: Addresses a widely felt inconvenience and boosts his appeal without clear harm.

Event: Gets sick of Selina, yet his anti-Meyer platform “works wonders” that suit her fine
Explanation: Advances his own career while ironically aiding Selina’s political situation.

Event: Is recruited to run for Congress as an easily replaceable placeholder
Explanation: He gains a powerful seat that others intended as temporary, complicating Jeff’s succession plan.

Event: Hangs onto positions he doesn’t deserve for far longer than intended
Explanation: Enjoys prolonged status while impeding more competent replacements.

Event: Shuts down the government out of spite
Explanation: Gains attention and leverage while imposing broad costs on citizens and institutions.

Event: Sets himself up for a presidential run after shutdown
Explanation: Uses his notoriety to fuel higher ambitions, worsening political quality.

Event: Obsessively points out he works at the White House to others
Explanation: Inflates his ego and stature while mostly just annoying others.

Event: Engages in an anti-vax crusade
Explanation: Sows dangerous misinformation that hurts others and ultimately backfires on him.

Event: Continues anti-vax crusade leading to his father’s death (patricide)
Explanation: Directly causes his father’s death and deep personal loss without gaining anything durable.

Event: Hides that his cancer is in remission and shaves his head to look like a cancer patient
Explanation: Improves approval ratings through deception at the cost of public trust.

Event: Makes up acronyms like “VPOTUS,” “FDOTUS,” and “VPVP”
Explanation: Derives self-amusement and identity while irritating coworkers.

Event: Has a hair-trigger temper and throws fits when offended
Explanation: Damages relationships and reputation without real benefit.

Event: Runs for President as far-right conspiracy theorist in a Democratic primary
Explanation: Gains frontrunner status while degrading party coherence and reality-based debate.

Event: Smugly rubs it in when he gets one over on Dan, including after Dan’s anxiety attack
Explanation: Enjoys dominance over Dan while worsening Dan’s distress and their working relationship.

Event: Throws his “Jeffersons” caucus against the bipartisan budget bill purely out of spite
Explanation: Gains spiteful satisfaction and notoriety while disrupting governance.

Event: Obfuscates his reliance on nepotism and claims he wants a job “on his own merits” via Uncle Jeff
Explanation: Seeks personal advancement while leaning on family and being self-deceiving.

Event: Gets banned from Uber due to low rating
Explanation: His bad behavior costs him a service while modestly protecting drivers from him.

Event: Frequently calls his mom
Explanation: Maintains supportive family connection that benefits both.

Event: Runs for office while drunk on power and increasingly vile
Explanation: Converts his ambition into influence that harms political culture.

Event: Goes on an anti-vax crusade that eventually contributes to losing his other testicle to cancer
Explanation: His broader reckless choices leave him severely physically harmed.

Event: Appreciates Richard and ensures he gets a cabinet position
Explanation: Uses his influence to reward a loyal friend, enabling Richard’s rise while reflecting well on himself.

Event: Ensures Richard gets a cabinet position that helps Richard become President
Explanation: Significantly advances Richard’s path to high office while associating himself with that success.

Event: Gives an epic denouncement of Teddy as soulless sexual harasser
Explanation: Accurately calls out abuse, helping the norm against harassment without clear self-gain.

Event: Has his anti-vax crusade lead to his father’s death specifically framed as patricide
Explanation: Direct killing-by-ideology harms both his family and himself with no benefit.

Event: Gets elected to Congress via anti-vax/anti-math/Islamophobic platforms
Explanation: Reaches high office through harmful, ignorant messaging that damages public understanding.

Event: Promises to ban math because he claims Algerians (Muslims) invented it and everyone hates Muslims
Explanation: Boosts support among extremists by attacking basic education and stigmatizing Muslims.

Event: Bans math rhetoric driven by a grudge against his math-teacher stepdad
Explanation: Uses public policy rhetoric to settle personal scores, harming educational norms.

Event: Frequently hits on Amy and Sue despite repeated rebuffs
Explanation: He wastes effort and worsens professional relations without ever succeeding.

Event: Is made Vice President with no power and later impeached
Explanation: He gains a title but ends powerless and impeached while enabling others’ strategies.

Event: Shows undying loyalty to Richard over time
Explanation: Consistent support fosters a mutually beneficial, stabilizing friendship.

Event: Wouldn’t care if Richard hit schoolchildren while driving; only cares about speed and filming
Explanation: Values his own agenda and impatience over children’s safety, though harm is narrowly avoided.
